place: Bargaintown CDP, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: Bargaintown earned its name when a developer sold lots cheaply after the town’s value failed to rise, making the area a bargain for buyers.
The Bargaintown Volunteer Fire Company #2, founded in 1932, still operates two stations—one on Mill Road and another on Tony Pagano Drive.
Geographically, Bargaintown sits at 39.3626°N latitude and –74.581°W longitude in Atlantic County, New Jersey.

The Greate Egg Harbour Township Historical Society Museum and Research Library preserves Egg Harbor Township history, tracing back to 1710 when the area encompassed all of present‑day Atlantic County.
The museum’s collections showcase artifacts that illustrate everyday life, events, and activities of Egg Harbor Township from its early days to today.
Bargaintown’s median age is 44.1 years, with roughly 24% of residents under 18 and 20% aged 65 or older, reflecting a balanced age spread.
